children.name: Chirodropus
children.description: Chirodropus — a genus of chirodropid box jellyfish in the family Chirodropidae.

children.name: Chirodropus gorilla
children.description: Chirodropus gorilla is a species of box jellyfish. It is one of two species in the genus Chirodropus. It lives in the deep waters of the western African coast, particularly Namibia.

children.name: Chirodropus palmatus
children.description: Chirodropus palmatus is a species of box jellyfish. It is one of two species belonging to the genus Chirodropus.

children.name: Chironex
children.description: Chironex — a genus of chirodropid box jellyfish in the family Chirodropidae.

children.name: Chironex fleckeri
children.description: Chironex fleckeri, commonly known as the Australian box jelly and nicknamed the sea wasp, is a species of extremely venomous box jellyfish found in coastal waters from northern Australia and Papua New Guinea to Indonesia, East Timor, Cambodia, Malaysia, Singapore, the Philippines and Vietnam. It has been described as "the most lethal jellyfish in the world", with at least 64 known deaths in Australia from 1884 to 2021.

children.name: Chironex indrasaksajiae
children.description: Chironex indrasaksajiae — a species in the genus Chironex, family Chirodropidae. Also known as chirodropid box jellyfish.

children.name: Chironex yamaguchii
children.description: Chironex yamaguchii, commonly known as ハブクラゲ and as "hub jellyfish" due to erroneous machine translations, is a species of box jellyfish found in coastal waters around Japan and the Philippines. Discovered in 2009, it is highly venomous and has been the cause of several deaths in Japanese waters.

children.name: Chirodectes
children.description: Chirodectes — a genus of chirodropid box jellyfish in the family Chirodropidae.

children.name: Chirodectes maculatus
children.description: Chirodectes maculatus — a species in the genus Chirodectes, family Chirodropidae. Also known as chirodropid box jellyfish.
